days.title: The Landing & The Underground City
days.startReason: Arrival day — you land at Haneda at 3:25 PM. Zero complexity goal after a 14-hour flight.
days.dayNotes: Goal: zero complexity. Private transfer to hotel, drop bags, then a 4-min walk into Tokyo Station's underground for world-class ramen. The 7-Eleven is right in your hotel building for late-night snacks.

stops.name: Haneda Airport Terminal 3
stops.notes: Meet your Klook driver at the arrivals hall. Look for a sign with your name.

stops.name: Courtyard by Marriott Tokyo Station
stops.notes: Yaesu side of Tokyo Station. Check in, drop bags, freshen up. 7-Eleven is in the building.

stops.name: Tokyo Character Street
stops.notes: In the Tokyo Station basement. Official Pokémon Center and Ghibli store — perfect first stop for Sadie.

stops.name: Rokurinsha (Ramen Street)
stops.notes: Thick dipping-style Tsukemen ramen. Open 7:30 AM–10:30 PM, last order 10:00 PM. Expect a short queue.

stops.name: Tsuru Ton Tan BIS TOKYO
stops.notes: Other option for dinner - Udon at Marunouchi Underground South Exit in Tokyo Station.

transit.notes: Driver meets you at arrivals. Drop-off at hotel lobby.
Walk north to Tokyo Station Yaesu South Exit. Follow signs for 'Tokyo Ichibangai' (First Avenue), then Ramen Street.

tickets.name: Klook Limo Transfer — Haneda to Hotel
tickets.notes: Pre-booked via Klook. Driver meets at Terminal 3 arrivals hall.

days.title: The Fish Market & The Anime Mecca
days.startReason: Tsukiji is best before 9 AM when stalls are freshest. Heading south early lets you beat the rush hour crowd heading into the city center.
days.rushHourNote: Avoid Tokyo trains 7:30–9:30 AM (you'll be walking to Tsukiji). Return from Akihabara before 5:30 PM or after 8 PM to skip evening rush.
days.dayNotes: Morning at Tsukiji, then a short walk to Age.3 Ginza for their famous fried cream bread before heading into Akihabara for the anime deep-dive. Stick to the Hibiya and Yamanote lines — simple and direct.

stops.name: Courtyard by Marriott Tokyo Station
stops.notes: Leave hotel early for Tsukiji.

stops.name: Tsukiji Outer Market
stops.notes: Walk 15 min south or take a 5-min taxi (GO app). Fresh sushi, tamagoyaki, and street food. Browse the stalls.

stops.name: Age.3 Ginza
stops.notes: 10-min walk north from Tsukiji. Famous fluffy fried bread filled with cream — a Tokyo signature treat. Then continue a few minutes to Ginza Station for the Hibiya Line to Akihabara. (1-24-11 Ginza, near Ginza-itchome Station)

stops.name: Radio Kaikan — Akihabara
stops.notes: Floors 4–6: Hatsune Miku and anime merchandise. Right outside the Electric Town Exit.

stops.name: Kanda Myojin Shrine
stops.notes: 10-min walk from Radio Kaikan. The 'geek shrine' — buy talismans for your electronics. Beautiful vermilion gates.

stops.name: Ichiran Shimbashi
stops.notes: Solo ramen booths — each person eats in their own cubicle. Customise your broth on a paper form.

transit.notes: Walk south. Or use GO app for a quick taxi.
Walk north to Age.3 (1-24-11 Ginza, near Ginza-itchome). Grab the famous fried cream bread, then continue a few minutes to Ginza Station for the Hibiya Line.
Direct line from Ginza. Exit at Akihabara — use Electric Town Exit for Radio Kaikan.
Only 2 stops. First train 4:30 AM, last train 12:30 AM.
Kyobashi Station is a 1-min walk from the hotel lobby. Direct to Shimbashi.

days.title: Tokyo DisneySea
days.startReason: Bus departs from Yaesu South Exit Bus Stop #1. Buses run every 10–20 min from 7:00 AM. Leave hotel by 7:30 AM to arrive at park near opening and beat the entry queues.
days.rushHourNote: Leaving at 7:30 AM means you travel before peak rush hour (7:30–9:30 AM) — it'll be moderately busy but you'll be heading against the main commuter flow.
days.dayNotes: DisneySea is legendary but transit is tricky. Take the bus from Yaesu South Exit — NOT the train (the Disney platform is a 20-min maze inside the station). Meals at quick service. Keep checking for last-minute restaurant reservations in the app.

stops.name: Courtyard by Marriott Tokyo Station
stops.notes: Leave by 7:30 AM. Walk north to Yaesu South Exit.

stops.name: Tokyo Station — Yaesu South Exit Bus Stop #1
stops.notes: Board the direct bus to Tokyo DisneySea here. Runs every 10–20 min, ~35 min ride.

stops.name: Tokyo DisneySea
stops.notes: Full day at the park! Meals at quick service. Keep scoping last-minute dining reservations.

stops.name: Maihama Station
stops.notes: Take the Disney Resort Line (monorail) from the park to Maihama Station.

transit.notes: Runs every 10–20 min from 7:00–10:00 AM. Much easier than the train.
Take the monorail around the resort to Maihama Station.
Direct train back. Runs until midnight. Park usually empties by 9:30 PM.

tickets.name: Tokyo DisneySea Entry Tickets
tickets.notes: Must be pre-purchased. Book well in advance — often sells out. Use the Disney app or Klook.

days.title: Harajuku Alternative & Shibuya Lights
days.startReason: CAFE REISSUE opens at 10:00 AM. Arrive by 9:45 AM to beat the 2-hour line. Saturday means no rush hour but crowds build quickly after noon.
days.rushHourNote: Saturday — no commuter rush. But Takeshita Street becomes shoulder-to-shoulder after noon. Arrive early.
days.dayNotes: Hit the 3D coffee first to beat the queue, then shop for Sadie's goth/emo style. Stroll via Cat Street to Shibuya for lunch and afternoon. Yakitori dinner in Nishiazabu — a polished, smoke-free spot.

stops.name: CAFE REISSUE — Harajuku
stops.notes: Arrive 15 min before 10 AM opening to beat the queue. Show the barista a photo of Sadie's favourite anime character for 3D foam art.

stops.name: Takeshita Street
stops.notes: 6-min walk from the cafe. Pedestrian-only street — the heart of Harajuku youth fashion.

stops.name: ACDC RAG 原宿本店
stops.notes: Yami Kawaii and goth-punk styles Sadie will love. On Takeshita Street.

stops.name: Totti Candy Factory
stops.notes: 1-min walk away. Famous 'Harajuku Rainbow' cotton candy — larger than an umbrella. Great photo.

stops.name: Katsudon-ya Zuicho — Shibuya
stops.notes: 20-min stroll south via Cat Street corridor. Opens 11:30 AM. Counter-only. Crispy pork katsudon — arrive near opening.

stops.name: INITIAL Omotesando
stops.notes: 17-min walk. 'Shime Parfaits' — artistic high-end desserts with seasonal fruit and Hokkaido cream.

stops.name: Shibuya 109
stops.notes: 15-min walk. 10-story tower — epicentre of Japanese teen fashion with alt and anime-influenced styles.

stops.name: Shibuya Parco
stops.notes: If you have extra time. Nintendo and Pokémon stores inside.

stops.name: Yakitori Toriyoshi Nishiazabu
stops.notes: Arrive ~6:00–6:15 PM, expect 30-min wait. Polished yakitori — calm, minimalist, smoke-free. 'Omakase stop system' keeps skewers coming until you say stop.

transit.notes: From Tokyo Station. Then 10-min scenic walk to CAFE REISSUE.
Trendy Cat Street corridor heading south — worth the stroll.
Rest your feet — take a taxi.
Direct taxi back to Courtyard Marriott.

days.title: The Master Knife & Digital Dreamscapes
days.startReason: Take the Ginza Line from Kyobashi Station to Tawaramachi — arriving at Kama-Asa around 10:00 AM when the knife shop opens. After Asakusa, a detour to Higashiueno for pearl jewelry. teamLab at 3:00 PM is ideal for a Sunday.
days.rushHourNote: Sunday — no rush hour. teamLab Borderless requires a specific entry time — book the 3:00 PM slot in advance.
days.dayNotes: From high-craft knife shopping in Kappabashi to Tokyo's oldest temple, then a detour to Higashiueno for pearl jewellery (for Sara), into the neon art of teamLab, and ending with A5 Wagyu in Ginza.

stops.name: Kama-Asa — Kappabashi
stops.notes: Handmade Damascus-style knives. On-site engraving available — perfect functional souvenir. Arrive at opening.

stops.name: Unatoto Asakusa
stops.notes: 10-min walk from the knife shops. Grilled eel (unagi) over rice — affordable, high-quality.

stops.name: Senso-ji Temple
stops.notes: 5-min walk. Tokyo's oldest temple. Explore the Nakamise shopping street leading to the gate.

stops.name: Asakusa Underground Mall
stops.notes: At the Ginza Line entrance. 1950s time-capsule vibe — surprisingly photogenic.

stops.name: PEARL SEIWA — Higashiueno
stops.notes: From Asakusa Station, take the Ginza Line 3 stops to Inaricho Station (3 min), then walk 5 min southwest. High-quality Japanese pearl jewellery — browse their collection and pick something for Sara. Allow about an hour.

stops.name: teamLab Borderless — Azabudai Hills
stops.notes: Booked 3:00 PM entry. Everything connected underground from Kamiyacho Station — follow signs for Azabudai Hills.

stops.name: Oumi Beef Yakiniku Nikutatsu — Ginza
stops.notes: A5 Wagyu with private booth seating. Tabelog reservation: 7ZSW7539A7. Take Hibiya Line back toward Ginza (~10 min).

stops.name: Itoya Stationery Store — Ginza
stops.notes: 12-story stationery store. Visit the indoor hydroponic farm on the top floor before or after dinner.

transit.notes: Kyobashi Station is 1 min from hotel lobby. Direct to Tawaramachi, then 5-min walk to Kama-Asa.
Easy flat walk south into Asakusa.
Board the Ginza Line at Asakusa, ride 3 stops to Inaricho Station. Walk 5 min southwest to PEARL SEIWA in Higashiueno.
Walk 6 min to Ueno-Okachimachi Station. Board the Hibiya Line (Gray) directly to Kamiyacho Station (no transfers, ~20 min). Follow underground signs for Azabudai Hills.
Head back toward Ginza on the Hibiya Line.
Easy walk back to Courtyard Marriott.

tickets.name: Oumi Beef Yakiniku Nikutatsu — Ginza
tickets.notes: 7:30 PM reservation (Tabelog). A5 Wagyu, private booth seating.

tickets.name: teamLab Borderless — 3:00 PM Entry
tickets.notes: Must book in advance. Entry time is strict. Located in Azabudai Hills, Mori Building.

days.title: Shinjuku Subcultures & Cosplay Adventures
days.startReason: Monday morning rush runs until ~9:30 AM. Leave after 10:00 AM to ride a comfortable train. Shinjuku Gyoen is best mid-morning.
days.rushHourNote: Avoid the Marunouchi Line before 9:30 AM on Monday — very crowded. Leave hotel at 10:00 AM.
days.dayNotes: Full day in Shinjuku. The cosplay adventure at 5 PM is the centrepiece — everything else builds around it. Tsurutontan is 4 mins from the Godzilla Head so dinner is effortless post-cosplay.

stops.name: Shinjuku Gyoen National Garden
stops.notes: Traditional Japanese, French, and English gardens — a peaceful contrast to the Shinjuku skyline. 5-min walk from station exit.

stops.name: Omoide Yokocho (Memory Lane)
stops.notes: 'Piss Alley' — narrow grid of tiny stalls serving yakitori and ramen. Look for the green and red lanterns next to the train tracks.

stops.name: Godzilla Head — Shinjuku Toho Building
stops.notes: Look up at the Kabukicho entrance. The Godzilla head looms over the building — unmissable. (1-19-1 Kabukicho)

stops.name: Don Quijote Shinjuku Kabukicho
stops.notes: Multi-story megastore. Perfect for unique snacks and souvenirs. (1-16-5 Kabukicho)

stops.name: 120-Minute Cosplay Adventure
stops.notes: Check in at 5:00 PM at Shinjuku Ale Bar — 1-14-5 Kabukicho, Shinjuku (Marui Building, 1F/2F). On Central Road heading toward the Godzilla Head, next to Karaoke 747. Guided walkthrough — dress as anime characters. Finishes around 7:00 PM. Booked via Viator (ref: 1386039823). ⚠️ Tickets available from May 30 once payment goes through — download before the day.

stops.name: Tsurutontan Shinjuku
stops.notes: 4-min walk from Godzilla Head. Stylish udon brasserie — non-smoking. Massive 'washbowl' sized udon bowls. Teenager-friendly with occasional live music.

transit.notes: Direct from Tokyo Station. Exit at Shinjuku-gyoemmae — garden is a 5-min walk.
Walk toward Shinjuku Station's West Exit for Memory Lane.
From Shinjuku Station directly back to Tokyo Station.

tickets.name: 120-Minute Cosplay Adventure
tickets.notes: Booked via Viator. 5:00 PM check-in at Shinjuku Ale Bar (upstairs), near the Godzilla Head, Kabukicho. Tickets available from May 30 once payment clears — download to your phone before June 1.

days.title: The Anime Flagship & Sky-High Sunshine City
days.startReason: CRITICAL: Luggage must be forwarded to Osaka by 10:00 AM. Drop bags at the bell desk first thing. Animate opens at 10:00 AM — arrive with time to spare.
days.rushHourNote: Tuesday rush 7:30–9:30 AM. Drop luggage at 8:30 AM, then wait until after 9:30 AM before boarding the Marunouchi Line.
days.dayNotes: Last full day in Tokyo. Forward luggage to Osaka via Takkyubin BEFORE 10 AM — critical for smooth departure tomorrow. The entire Sunshine 60 complex is a city within a city.

stops.name: Courtyard by Marriott — Bell Desk
stops.notes: ⚠️ DROP LARGE LUGGAGE FOR TAKKYUBIN FORWARDING TO OSAKA BY 10:00 AM. Ask the bell desk for the Takkyubin (luggage delivery) service.

stops.name: Animate Ikebukuro Flagship
stops.notes: World's largest anime shop — 9 floors of manga, CDs, exclusive merch, and a theatre. Often has exclusive pop-up shops. Opens 10:00 AM.

stops.name: Namja Gyoza Stadium — Sunshine 60
stops.notes: 6-min walk from Animate. Inside Namjatown (indoor theme park, 2nd floor of Sunshine 60). Try tasting plates from gyoza stalls across Japan. Non-smoking.

stops.name: Pokémon Center Mega Tokyo & Pikachu Sweets
stops.notes: One of the largest Pokémon Centres in the world. Pikachu Sweets has character-themed cupcakes and lattes.

stops.name: Donguri Kyowakoku — Studio Ghibli Shop
stops.notes: Within the Sunshine 60 mall. High-quality Ghibli merch — Totoro, Spirited Away, and more.

stops.name: Sunshine 60 Observatory Tenbou Park
stops.notes: 360-degree view from 251 metres up. Indoor grassy areas and café. Great last look at Tokyo.

stops.name: Seafood Buffet Dining Ginza Happo
stops.notes: King crab, fresh sushi, and premium yakiniku in an elegant, smoke-free basement setting. Tabelog reservation: 7P97W8MM6M. (GINZA NINE 3, B1 — south Ginza near Shimbashi)

transit.notes: Direct. Leave after 9:30 AM to avoid rush. 5-min walk from station to Animate.
All connected within the Sunshine City complex.
Higashi-Ikebukuro Station is connected to the Sunshine 60 mall. Direct to Ginza-itchome.
Last night at the Courtyard. Easy walk or quick taxi.

tickets.name: Seafood Buffet Dining Ginza Happo
tickets.notes: 7:30 PM reservation (Tabelog). GINZA NINE 3, B1F — south Ginza near Shimbashi.

tickets.name: Takkyubin Luggage Forwarding to Osaka
tickets.notes: Drop large bags at hotel bell desk BEFORE 10:00 AM. They will arrive at The Westin Osaka by the afternoon of June 3. Ask front desk to arrange.

days.title: The Mt. Fuji Shinkansen Run → Soul of Osaka
days.startReason: If Mt. Fuji is visible, take an earlier train and skip lunch. If not visible, enjoy Godaime Hanayama Udon (opens 11 AM) before boarding.
days.rushHourNote: Wednesday — leave hotel after 9:30 AM to avoid residual rush. Dotonbori gets crowded after 8 PM but the Haunted Castle tour is at 8 PM so plan around it.
days.dayNotes: The easiest travel day. Board the Nozomi Shinkansen — SEATS D AND E (right side from Tokyo) for the Mt. Fuji view ~45 min in. Check isfujivisible.com before choosing your train time.

stops.name: Godaime Hanayama Udon — Ginza
stops.notes: Optional if Fuji is NOT visible. Near Higashi-Ginza (behind Kabukiza Theatre), famous for extra-wide flat noodles in a crane-shaped bowl. Opens 11:00 AM.

stops.name: Tokyo Station — Shinkansen Gates
stops.notes: Board Nozomi Shinkansen bound for Shin-Osaka. ⚠️ SEATS D AND E — right-hand side when heading to Osaka. Mt. Fuji visible ~45 min in.

stops.name: Mt. Fuji Viewing Window
stops.notes: Look right from Seats D/E approximately 45 minutes into the journey. Check isfujivisible.com in the morning!

stops.name: Shin-Osaka Station
stops.notes: Arrive Shin-Osaka. Transfer to JR Kyoto Line (1 stop, 4 min) to JR Osaka Station.

stops.name: The Westin Osaka
stops.notes: Exit at Sakurabashi Exit and catch the Westin Shuttle Bus (every 15–20 min, 7:30 AM–10 PM). Or 10–12 min walk via Umeda Underground.

stops.name: Umeda Sky Building — Kuchu Teien Observatory
stops.notes: Two towers linked by a 'floating garden' observatory on the 39th floor — panoramic 360° views of Osaka. About 12 min walk northwest from the Westin. Open 10 AM–10 PM (last entry 9:30 PM). ¥2,000 per person. Head to Dotonbori afterwards.

stops.name: Dotonbori
stops.notes: Street food, shopping, and Osaka energy. 'Kuidaore' — eat until you drop!

stops.name: Wagyu Itaden
stops.notes: Amazing wagyu bento box. Alternatives: Mizuno for Okonomiyaki, Kukuru for Takoyaki.

stops.name: Osaka Castle — Haunted Tour
stops.notes: Meet at the BACK EXIT of Starbucks at Jo-Terrace, near OsakaJoKoen Station. Guide wears a GREEN BACKPACK. Booking ref: GYGG45XBK2NZ.

transit.notes: ⚠️ SEATS D & E (standard car) or Seat E (Green Car) — RIGHT side for Mt. Fuji. Book via SmartEX. Large luggage requires 'Seat with Oversized Luggage Area' reservation if bags >160cm total.
Just 1 stop.
Look for the High-Speed Bus Terminal area near Sakurabashi Exit. Every 15–20 min, 7:30 AM–10 PM.
Take shuttle to Osaka Station → walk 2 min to Umeda Station → Midosuji Line (Red) → Namba Station.
Taxi is easiest from Dotonbori to OsakaJoKoen Station area. Meet guide at Starbucks Jo-Terrace back exit.

tickets.name: Nozomi Shinkansen — Tokyo → Shin-Osaka
tickets.notes: Book via SmartEX app. Request Seats D & E (standard) or Seat E (Green Car). Right side = Mt. Fuji view. If bags are over 160cm total, must reserve 'Seat with Oversized Luggage Area'.

tickets.name: Haunted Osaka Castle Tour
tickets.notes: 8:00 PM. Meet at BACK EXIT of Starbucks at Jo-Terrace, near OsakaJoKoen Station. Guide wears GREEN BACKPACK.

days.title: Relaxing Day — Umeda & Omakase
days.startReason: Easy day — sleep in and explore on foot near the hotel. The only fixed commitment is the 6:00 PM omakase dinner.
days.rushHourNote: Thursday rush 7:30–9:30 AM — not relevant since you're starting late.
days.dayNotes: Chill day near the Westin. Sukiyaki and shabu-shabu lunch at KITTE Osaka — just an 8-min walk. The omakase at Sushi Fukushima is the centrepiece — VIP Course at 8:30 PM. Don't be late.

stops.name: The Westin Osaka
stops.notes: Relaxed morning. Enjoy the hotel or stroll the Nakanoshima area.

stops.name: Sukiyaki Shabushabu Tsukada — KITTE Osaka
stops.notes: Premium sukiyaki and shabu-shabu with wagyu, Kobe beef, and Matsusaka beef options. 5F of KITTE Osaka (former Osaka Central Post Office), directly connected to JR Osaka Station west exit — about 8 min walk from the Westin. (3-2-2 Umeda, Kita-ku)

stops.name: Sushi Fukushima — Osaka
stops.notes: 2-hour VIP Omakase Course. Do NOT be late. Tabelog reservation: 4NQSTAYVXR. (1-2-17 Fukushima, Fukushima-ku — west of central Osaka near Fukushima Station)

transit.notes: Walk south toward JR Osaka Station. KITTE Osaka is directly connected to the station's west exit — take the elevator to the 5th floor.
Recommended for a reservation dinner — don't risk being late. Restaurant is in Fukushima Ward, west of Umeda.

tickets.name: Sushi Fukushima — VIP Omakase Course
tickets.notes: 8:30 PM reservation (Tabelog). 2-hour course. Confirm details and any dietary notes in advance.

days.title: Kyoto Day Trip — Manga, Udon & Kiyamachi Nights
days.startReason: Need to arrive at the Manga Museum by 9:45 AM to be first in line for the Manga Portrait list. Leave by 8:45 AM to catch the JR Special Rapid.
days.rushHourNote: Friday — Osaka trains busy until ~9 AM. Catch the shuttle early and board the train. Last Special Rapid from Kyoto back to Osaka: 11:29 PM. Local trains until 12:14 AM.
days.dayNotes: A day in Kyoto's creative and culinary heart. Sign up for the Manga Portrait first thing — then explore while you wait. Everything is walkable in the city centre.

stops.name: Westin Osaka → Osaka Station
stops.notes: Take the Westin shuttle to Osaka Station.

stops.name: Kyoto International Manga Museum
stops.notes: Arrive by 9:45 AM — be first in line. Head IMMEDIATELY to the 'Manga Artist Corner' to sign up for the Manga Portrait list. Browse 50,000 volumes on the 'Wall of Manga' while you wait.

stops.name: Omen Ginkaku-ji
stops.notes: Artisan udon with a platter of seasonal condiments (ginger, sesame, Kyoto veg). 15-min taxi (GO app) or Bus #32/#203 to Ginkakuji-michi stop.

stops.name: Nishiki Market
stops.notes: 'Kitchen of Kyoto.' High-quality kitchenware and food souvenirs. Bus #5 or #203 from Ginkakuji back to Shijo-Kawaramachi.

stops.name: Teramachi Shopping Street
stops.notes: Covered arcade adjacent to Nishiki. Traditional woodblock prints, fine stationery, and tea merchants.

stops.name: Ninenzaka
stops.notes: If time allows — short taxi. Preserved wooden architecture from the Edo period. Beautiful for photos.

stops.name: Chao Chao Gyoza — Sanjo Kiyamachi
stops.notes: Award-winning bite-sized 'hitokuchi' gyoza. Unique flavours: shrimp, ginger, curry. 10-min walk from Teramachi/Nishiki.

stops.name: Kyoto Station
stops.notes: Walk 15 min or take subway from nearby Sanjo Station. Board JR Special Rapid back to Osaka.

transit.notes: Frequent departures. Runs throughout the day.
Then 2-min walk to the museum.
Taxi via GO app is most efficient. Or walk to main road and take Bus #32 or #203 to Ginkakuji-michi stop.
Back toward Nishiki Market and Teramachi.
Last Special Rapid: 11:29 PM. Local trains until 12:14 AM. Don't miss it!

days.title: Kobe Beef & Osaka Craftsmanship
days.startReason: Depart for Kobe at 11:00 AM. The JR Special Rapid to Sannomiya takes 22 minutes. Arrive in time for lunch at Ishida.
days.rushHourNote: Saturday — no rush hour. Evening trains from Namba back to Umeda are frequent. Last Midosuji: ~11:50 PM.
days.dayNotes: Luxury lunch in Kobe, then back to Osaka for knife shopping, fashion for Sadie in Amemura, retro Shinsekai vibes, and Italian dinner at MAKIBI for a welcome change of pace.

stops.name: Sannomiya Station — Kobe
stops.notes: JR Special Rapid from Osaka Station (22 min). 5-min walk to Ishida.

stops.name: Kobe Beef Steak Ishida Main Shop
stops.notes: A5 Kobe beef prepared teppanyaki-style right in front of you. Your locked-in Kobe experience. Tabelog reservation: 4EMQNJYCCP.

stops.name: Sennichimae Doguyasuji — Knife Street
stops.notes: 10-min walk south from Namba Station. Look for Sakai Ichimonji Mitsuhide — Damascus-style blades with on-site engraving. Name or date engraved as permanent souvenir.

stops.name: Amerikamura (Amemura) — Triangle Park
stops.notes: 15-min walk or quick subway hop from Doguyasuji to Shinsaibashi. Heart of Osaka youth culture. Volcan & Aphrodite: bold graphic tees and alt styles for Sadie.

stops.name: Tsutenkaku — Shinsekai
stops.notes: Observatory deck for city views. Or try the 'Tower Slider' — 60-metre slide spiralling down the outside of the tower. (1-18-6 Ebisuhigashi, Naniwa Ward)

stops.name: Kasuga Goraku-jo Vintage Arcade
stops.notes: Vintage arcade near Tsutenkaku capturing the 'retro-future' look.

stops.name: MAKIBI — Italian
stops.notes: Tabelog Italian WEST Top 100 2025 (rated 3.67). Wood-fired pizza, seasonal Italian with open-fire meat dishes — a perfect break from Japanese food. Tabelog reservation: 5CSD7KLD5F. 1F Mobius Nishitenma Building, 3-1-27 Nishitenma, Kita-ku. ~6 min walk from Naniwabashi Station.

transit.notes: Direct from Osaka Station platform.
Head back after lunch.
South toward Namba.
To Dobutsuen-mae Station. Shinsekai is right there.
Easiest for an 8 PM reservation — taxi direct from Shinsekai to 3-1-27 Nishitenma, Kita-ku. Or: Midosuji Line north to Yodoyabashi, then 10-min walk east.
Nishitenma is about 1km south of the Westin — pleasant late-night walk through Umeda, or a quick taxi.

tickets.name: Kobe Beef Steak Ishida — Lunch
tickets.notes: 12:00 PM reservation (Tabelog). Teppanyaki-style A5 Kobe beef.

tickets.name: MAKIBI — Italian Dinner
tickets.notes: 8:00 PM reservation (Tabelog). 1F Mobius Nishitenma Building, 3-1-27 Nishitenma, Kita-ku, Osaka.

days.title: Second Kyoto Day — Omurice, Pontocho & Lantern Alleys
days.startReason: ⚠️ BOOK KICHI KICHI OMURICE at 9:00 AM SHARP from hotel. Same-day online booking only — slots sell out in minutes. Have the booking page ready on your phone at 8:59 AM.
days.rushHourNote: Sunday — no rush. Must depart Kyoto by 3:45 PM to be back in Osaka by 5 PM for the 7 PM food tour.
days.dayNotes: Start the morning by booking Kichi Kichi at 9 AM on the dot, then take the Hankyu Kyoto Line (NOT JR) to Kyoto-Kawaramachi. Omurice lunch, Pontocho afternoon stroll, lantern arcade, and back to Osaka for Fay's food tour. The Hankyu line from Osaka-Umeda is faster and cheaper than JR for this route.

stops.name: Osaka-Umeda Station (Hankyu)
stops.notes: Walk or shuttle from Westin to Hankyu Osaka-Umeda Station. Board the Limited Express (特急) on the Hankyu Kyoto Line toward Kyoto-Kawaramachi.

stops.name: Kichi Kichi Omurice
stops.notes: ⚠️ Arrive 20 min early and stand on the RED LINE painted on the pavement outside. Chef Yuichi Goto's theatrical omurice — a showstopper. Zaimokucho, 7-min walk north from Kyoto-Kawaramachi Station.

stops.name: Pontocho Alley
stops.notes: Narrow cobblestone lane running parallel to the Kamo River. One of Kyoto's most atmospheric alleys — quiet in the afternoon, magical at dusk.

stops.name: Kamogawa Riverfront
stops.notes: Stroll along the Kamo River. Couples famously sit at equal intervals along the bank. Peaceful contrast between modern city life and Kyoto's natural setting.

stops.name: Teramachi & Shinkyogoku Shopping Arcades
stops.notes: Covered arcade running north–south through the city centre. Traditional crafts, souvenir shops, fashion, and street food. Connects to Nishiki Market.

stops.name: Furukawacho Shopping Arcade
stops.notes: East side of the Kamo River — the famous lantern-hung arcade. Great photo spot. 5-min walk or quick taxi from Teramachi.

stops.name: Kyoto-Kawaramachi Station (return)
stops.notes: ⚠️ DEPART BY 15:45 on the Hankyu Kyoto Line Limited Express back to Osaka-Umeda (45 min, ¥410). Must be back in Osaka by 5 PM to rest before the 7 PM food tour.

stops.name: 10 Tastings of Osaka Food Tour
stops.notes: With Fay from withLocals. Booking reference: 7D89B827. Meet near Dotonbori at 7:00 PM — confirm exact meeting point with Fay.

transit.notes: Walk south to Hankyu Osaka-Umeda Station, or take the Westin shuttle to JR Osaka Station and walk 5 min to the Hankyu building.

transit.method: Hankyu Kyoto Line — Limited Express (特急)
transit.notes: NOT JR — use the Hankyu Kyoto Line from Osaka-Umeda. Take the Limited Express (特急) direct to Kyoto-Kawaramachi. Departs every ~10 min.
Walk 7 min north to Zaimokucho. Google Maps: 'Kichi Kichi Omurice Kyoto.'

transit.method: Hankyu Kyoto Line — Limited Express (特急)
transit.notes: Depart no later than 15:45. Same station, opposite platform — direction Osaka-Umeda.
Shuttle to Osaka Station → walk 2 min to Umeda → Midosuji Line south → Namba.

tickets.name: Kichi Kichi Omurice — Same-Day Booking
tickets.notes: Book online at 9:00 AM SHARP on June 7. Same-day only — no advance booking. Slots sell out in minutes. Have the booking page open on your phone at 8:59 AM.

tickets.name: 10 Tastings of Osaka — withLocals (Fay)
tickets.notes: 7:00 PM. Confirm exact meeting point with Fay ahead of time. Walking food tour of Dotonbori area.

days.title: Alternative Culture & Shinsaibashi Style
days.startReason: Monday rush until ~9:30 AM. Leave hotel at 10:00 AM after rush clears.
days.rushHourNote: Monday — avoid Midosuji Line before 9:30 AM. Last Midosuji from Honmachi toward Umeda: ~12:06 AM.
days.dayNotes: Full immersion in Osaka's youth subcultures. Amemura for Sadie, Shinsaibashi for shopping, then Osaka-style unagi for dinner. The Osaka-style unagi (direct charcoal, no steaming first) is crispier and richer than Tokyo-style.

stops.name: Sankaku Koen — Triangle Park (Amemura)
stops.notes: Epicentre of Osaka street fashion. Shinsaibashi Station, Exit 7 or 8.

stops.name: Alice On Wednesday
stops.notes: Boutique themed after Alice in Wonderland with a 'goth-cute' aesthetic. Perfect for Sadie. (2-12-25 Nishishinsaibashi, Chuo-ku)

stops.name: Mandarake Grand Chaos
stops.notes: Multi-story temple for otaku culture. Rare anime figures, alternative subculture goods. NOTE: Located in Nipponbashi (Den Den Town), not Amemura — 15-min walk south or quick taxi. (4-12-6 Nippombashi, Naniwa-ku)

stops.name: Red Rock Amemura (or Critters Burger)
stops.notes: Option A — Red Rock: viral 'Roast Beef Don' — mountain of beef over rice with creamy yogurt sauce and raw egg yolk. Option B — Critters Burger: best gourmet burger in Osaka, Japanese beef on artisanal buns. (2-10-21 Nishishinsaibashi, Chuo-ku)

stops.name: Shinsaibashi-Suji Shopping Street
stops.notes: 5-min walk east from Amemura. Nearly 1 mile of covered shopping. Sanrio Gallery for character goods; huge drugstores for Japanese skincare and alt makeup.

stops.name: Yoshitora — Honmachi
stops.notes: Premier Osaka-style unagi. Grilled directly over charcoal without steaming — crispier skin, richer flavour. Midosuji Line north 1 stop to Honmachi (2 min), Exit 12, 5-min walk. (1-6-6 Bingomachi, Chuo Ward)

transit.notes: Shuttle to Osaka Station → Midosuji Line south → Shinsaibashi Station (6 min). Use Exit 7 or 8.
Honmachi Station, Exit 12, 5-min walk.
2 stops north to Umeda Station. Last Midosuji from Honmachi: ~12:06 AM. Trains every 4–6 min.

days.title: To Haneda — The Final Run
days.startReason: Leave hotel ~11:00 AM. Need time to reach Shin-Osaka for the Shinkansen. If using Takkyubin, bags should already be at the Haneda hotel — just carry day bags.
days.rushHourNote: Tuesday — Osaka trains moderately busy 7:30–9:30 AM. Leave after 9:30 AM.
days.dayNotes: The most efficient route: Westin → Osaka Station → Shin-Osaka → Nozomi Shinkansen → Shinagawa → Keikyu Airport Line → Haneda Terminal 3. The Royal Park Hotel is literally inside Terminal 3 on the 3rd floor.

stops.name: The Westin Osaka
stops.notes: Final check of luggage. If using Takkyubin, bags should already be en route to Haneda. Carry only hand luggage.

stops.name: Shin-Osaka Station — Shinkansen
stops.notes: Board Nozomi Shinkansen to Shinagawa. ⚠️ SEATS D AND E for Mt. Fuji view on the left (~45 min in).

stops.name: Mt. Fuji Viewing Window (return trip)
stops.notes: Look LEFT from Seats D/E on the return journey to Tokyo.

stops.name: Shinagawa Station
stops.notes: Arrive from Shin-Osaka. Transfer to the Keikyu Airport Line on the same level.

stops.name: Haneda Airport Terminal 3
stops.notes: Keikyu Airport Line Ltd. Express, ~13 min. Last stop: Haneda Airport Terminal 3.

stops.name: Royal Park Hotel Tokyo Haneda
stops.notes: Directly inside Terminal 3 — 3rd floor. Walk straight from the hotel lobby to check-in counters tomorrow. Note: Hotel Villa Fontaine Premier is also connected via Haneda Airport Garden (rooftop spa option).

stops.name: Haneda Airport Garden — Dinner
stops.notes: Massive connected complex with dozens of high-end restaurant options. The perfect final dinner before your flight home.

transit.notes: Shuttle to Sakurabashi Exit or taxi direct.
Just 1 stop on the local JR line.
SEATS D & E for Mt. Fuji view. Book via SmartEX.
Direct to Haneda Airport Terminal 3 Station.

tickets.name: Nozomi Shinkansen — Shin-Osaka → Shinagawa
tickets.notes: Book via SmartEX. SEATS D & E for Mt. Fuji. Pick up Ekiben (station bento) at Shin-Osaka Eki Marché before boarding — look for 'Kobe Beef' or 'Osaka-style' box.

tickets.name: Takkyubin Luggage — Westin to Haneda Hotel
tickets.notes: If arranged, bags arrive at Royal Park Hotel by afternoon of June 9. Confirm with Westin bell desk on June 8.
